posts.text: USPS has hit its $15B borrowing limit and holds about one month of cash. Elena Patel walks through the structural mismatch between the universal service mandate and how it's financed—and what Congress could do about it. brook.gs/4soPvak

Two weeks into the war with Iran, the regime has taken hits—but it's holding. No major defections, a new supreme leader, and an uncertain endgame for the Trump administration.

For The Current podcast, Suzanne Maloney, Mara Karlin, and Aslı Aydıntaşbaş assess the state of the conflict. brook.gs/4ruRYiq

As federal gridlock persists, states are diverging into two distinct governance models. From abortion to elections, Elaine Kamarck argues that the "laboratories of democracy" are becoming a partisan patchwork, offering a national safety valve, but at a high cost to unified rights. brook.gs/4b3vu34

Introducing Uprooted: Rediscovering American History—a new series reclaiming the stories of marginalized communities being erased from our national narrative. The first cycle, focused on migration and immigration, launches end of March. Read the introduction and sign up for updates ⏬ brook.gs/4ljcPnn

How will AI reshape U.S. national security, workers, and infrastructure?

Join us Wednesday at 11a ET for a fireside chat with @SenMarkKelly, followed by an expert panel on AI in military and defense applications. Attend online or in person 👇 brook.gs/4rkavOa

This International Women's Day, Brookings Echidna Global Scholars reflect on what it takes to move from policy commitments to lived equality—and why that requires redesigning systems, not just enduring them. brook.gs/4rbZKO9

New research from Annelies Goger finds that, despite support for expanding apprenticeships in the U.S., core obstacles remain. "To scale participation, policymakers need to make structural changes—lowering the barriers without sacrificing rigor and quality," she writes. brook.gs/47fJR1O

Could Texas flip the Senate? After Talarico's primary win and Cornyn's near-upset, Brookings' Bill Galston breaks down why the Lone Star State's May GOP runoff may help decide who controls the Senate in 2027. brook.gs/47fRTaY
What's next for the Iran war? For The Current, four Brookings experts discuss the evolving conflict and its implications for the country, the Middle East, and international security. brook.gs/3NffFND

What is the endgame in Iran? @BrookingsFP visiting fellow Mara Karlin highlights 3 things to watch for in the coming days.

Video

2026’s joint review of the USMCA will shape the future of North American trade and competitiveness. The USMCA Forward 2026 report brings together expert analysis on what is working in the agreement—and where reforms are needed across key sectors and labor. brook.gs/4aMriog

What happens next in Iran? Experts across Brookings explain what the U.S. and Israel joint military operation and resulting missile attacks in the region mean for Iran's future and international/domestic security. brook.gs/40DREmj

Jeffrey Feltman reflects on being one of the few Americans ever to meet Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei—offering insights into how Khamenei viewed Iran’s place in the world. brook.gs/4aJO8wO
Hear more of the January 22 podcast episode on Iran here: brookings.edu/articles/irans…

On Saturday, President Trump announced that the U.S-Israeli attacks on Iran had killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. In January on the Current podcast, @asliaydintasbas asked Iran expert @MaloneySuzanne what would happen if Khamenei died in a U.S. bombing campaign.

Video
On Tuesday, hear from Brookings foreign policy experts on the war in Iran. Register to watch online. brookings.edu/events/us-iran…
